label: SES-047 governance schema-design workstream established: 1 session, 0 decisions, 0 planning items (PI-022 already exists from SES-046), 1 reference

session.title: Governance entity schema-design workstream established; workstream master plan, methodology guide, and six per-entity schema-design kickoff prompts produced
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai workstream-establishing planning conversation for the governance entity schema-design workstream. Opened against PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-entity-schema-workstream-establishing-kickoff.md (the kickoff committed at e1a0be4 as part of SES-046's artifacts). Conversation produced eight artifacts in the order specified by the kickoff: the workstream master plan, the schema-spec methodology guide, and the six per-entity schema-design kickoff prompts (workstream, conversation, reference book, work ticket, close-out payload, deposit event). The kickoff anticipated this conversation would also formalize six principle-level decisions and one retroactive-backfill planning item; pre-flight against the live database resolved that DEC-117..122 and PI-022 had already been recorded by the predecessor scoping conversation SES-046, so this conversation referenced them as the foundation rather than re-recording them. One mid-conversation correction was made: the workstream plan and the deposit-event kickoff initially claimed this conversation authored the backfill planning item; both were updated in a follow-up commit to cite PI-022 by identifier. No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Establish the governance entity schema-design workstream and produce its scaffolding artifacts. Four categories of deliverable: (1) Six new decision records formalizing the six incoming principles below (numbers in the Decision 117 range; actual values resolved at conversation close); (2) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-schema-workstream-plan.md — workstream master plan; (3) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md — methodology guide; (4) Six kickoff prompts under PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/, one per per-entity schema-design conversation."

Topics worked through:

1. Pre-flight resolution against the live database. The kickoff was authored under the assumption that the predecessor scoping conversation had not yet recorded the six principle-level decisions and the backfill planning item at this conversation's open. Pre-flight resolved that SES-046 had in fact closed out earlier the same day with DEC-117..122 and PI-022 already recorded. Treating those records as the workstream's foundation rather than re-recording reduced the deliverable count from four categories to three (master plan, methodology guide, six kickoff prompts) plus a session record.

2. Master plan drafting. Modeled on methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (SES-011's analogue). Sections cover purpose, origin and foundation (referencing DEC-117..122 explicitly), scope (six entities under minimum-viable framing, with the six identifier-prefix working assumptions: WS, CONV, RB, WT, COP, DEP), methodology (eight-conversation workstream: this conversation + six schema-design + one build-planning), workstream structure with sequence diagram, independence (per DEC-122, does not gate on CBM PI-001 or multi-tenancy routing fix), bootstrap (the chicken-and-egg about designing workstream and conversation entities while operating inside a workstream and conversations that have no database records — resolved by deferring retroactive population to PI-022, executed after build ships), governance (no new decisions or planning items authored here; refers to DEC-117..122 and PI-022), architectural disciplines (vocabulary discipline per access/vocab.py, API envelope per the SES-025 fix, client-side identifier computation, per-engagement isolation, source-first relationship-kind naming per DEC-048, parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046), open questions, link-outs to companion documents, glossary.

3. Schema-spec methodology guide drafting. Modeled on methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md. Inherits parent-prefix field naming (DEC-046) and source-first relationship-kind naming (DEC-048) as policy for new entity types regardless of category — no methodology-only caveat, in contrast to the methodology guide's section 6 forward-only scope. PI-006 retrofit of existing governance entities remains tracked separately and untouched. Adds append-only as a first-class lifecycle option in section 3.4 (deposit event will likely need it; close-out payload may); methodology guide did not have this. Lifecycle posture inherited as 'prefer simple,' but conversation entity gets the full seven-state DEC-119 lifecycle explicitly as a tolerated exception. Section 6 cross-spec consistency table covers identifier prefix style, status field naming, status casing, relationship-kind naming, field naming, timestamp inheritance, soft-delete versus append-only, identifier auto-assignment, lifecycle preference, API envelope.

4. Six per-entity kickoff prompts drafted. Workstream order: workstream → conversation → reference book → work ticket → close-out payload → deposit event. Each follows the schema-design-kickoff-domain.md model from the methodology workstream: task, context (why this entity at this position), what the entity needs to host with real grounding examples from the current project state, read-first list, architectural questions likely to arise, working style, pre-flight, governance at close, what the conversation does NOT do. Grounding examples drawn from real project artifacts: actual workstreams (methodology, v0.5 engagement-management, v0.6 styling, multi-tenancy routing fix, CBM paper test, this one), actual reference books (PRDs v0.1..v0.6, implementation plans, master plans, methodology guides, the conduct framework, the multi-engagement architecture document), actual work tickets (the methodology workstream's per-entity kickoffs and this workstream's seven kickoffs, the Claude Code apply prompts), actual close-out payload files at close-out-payloads/. The deposit-event kickoff (sixth) has one extra deliverable at close — the build-planning conversation's kickoff prompt — called out explicitly so it is not lost in closeout.

5. Identifier prefix working assumptions named but not locked. WS, CONV, RB, WT, COP, DEP carried through the kickoff prompts as working assumptions. Each per-entity conversation has first refusal at lock-in; the spec guide's section 6 collision list will be amended as each conversation closes.

6. Mid-conversation correction (committed separately). Initial drafts of the workstream plan and deposit-event kickoff stated that this conversation authors the retroactive-backfill planning item. Pre-flight had already established PI-022 exists; the claim slipped through into drafting. Five passages in the workstream plan and one passage in the deposit-event kickoff updated to cite PI-022 by identifier and attribute its authorship to SES-046. Two follow-up file changes, one follow-up commit.

7. Bootstrap framing explicit in master plan section 7. This workstream designs the workstream and conversation entities, but the conversations doing the designing are themselves conversations inside a workstream that has no records. Resolution: during the workstream, the session record on the append-only sessions table remains the only governance record per conversation per DEC-013; the workstream itself has no database record during its lifetime; retroactive population is PI-022's responsibility, executed after the build ships. Each schema-design conversation designs without depending on backfill ordering, but the schemas must support retroactive population (records insertable with historical timestamps and complete status).

8. Eight artifacts produced over four local commits, pushed to origin/main after sign-off: governance-schema-workstream-plan.md, governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md, six schema-design-kickoff-{entity}.md files. Plus one corrective commit for the PI-022 framing.
session.artifacts_produced:
Eight files committed to PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ in the crmbuilder repo over five commits (95b7939, 802bdc4, 369f32e, 0dfcc25, 0aeb5c0):

(1) governance-schema-workstream-plan.md (new, v1.0) — workstream master plan, 301 lines. Establishes the workstream's purpose, references DEC-117..122 as foundation and PI-022 as the backfill question, names six per-entity schema-design conversations in workstream order followed by a build-planning conversation, captures the bootstrap chicken-and-egg framing, lists architectural disciplines each per-entity conversation must apply, glossary.

(2) governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (new, v1.0) — schema-spec methodology guide, 343 lines. Template every per-entity schema specification follows: nine required sections (identity, fields, relationships, lifecycle, API surface, UI considerations, acceptance criteria, open questions, cross-references), conversation cadence (pre-flight reads, one-question-at-a-time discussion, drafting, governance recording at close), cross-spec consistency conventions, two validation gates.

(3) schema-design-kickoff-workstream.md (new) — kickoff for conversation 1 of 6. Working prefix WS. Includes nested-workstream question that DEC-120 deferred to this entity's schema-design conversation.

(4) schema-design-kickoff-conversation.md (new) — kickoff for conversation 2 of 6. Working prefix CONV. Carries the seven-state lifecycle from DEC-119 as the working starting point.

(5) schema-design-kickoff-reference-book.md (new) — kickoff for conversation 3 of 6. Working prefix RB. Versioning model is the principal design question; references charter and status entities' versioned-replace pattern as the closest existing model.

(6) schema-design-kickoff-work-ticket.md (new) — kickoff for conversation 4 of 6. Working prefix WT. Boundary question against reference book (single-use vs. continued-reference) is the principal design question.

(7) schema-design-kickoff-close-out-payload.md (new) — kickoff for conversation 5 of 6. Working prefix COP. Payload-content representation (JSON column, text column, file-path-plus-metadata, or hybrid) is the principal design question. Append-only after apply is the working assumption.

(8) schema-design-kickoff-deposit-event.md (new) — kickoff for conversation 6 of 6, and the workstream's last schema-design conversation. Working prefix DEP. Back-references to records the apply wrote (references-table edges vs. JSON column vs. dedicated table vs. target-side columns) is the principal design question. Append-only throughout. This conversation also produces an additional artifact at close: the build-planning conversation's kickoff prompt at governance-schema-build-planning-kickoff.md.
session.in_flight_at_end: Next workstream conversation: workstream entity schema design. Opens against PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/schema-design-kickoff-workstream.md. That conversation produces governance-schema-specs/workstream.md (creating the governance-schema-specs/ directory in the process), settles the nested-workstream question DEC-120 deferred, locks the WS identifier prefix, and establishes the cross-spec consistency precedents the remaining five schema-design conversations follow. After all six schema-design conversations complete, the build-planning conversation opens against governance-schema-build-planning-kickoff.md (drafted at the close of the deposit-event conversation) and produces the governance entity Product Requirements Document, implementation plan, and per-slice build prompts. Target user-interface version is open at this writing; the build-planning conversation sets it in coordination with the active version sequence at that time. Cleveland Business Mentors redo Phase 1 conversation continues to wait on Planning Item 001 (sub-domain hierarchy amendment) in the Cleveland Business Mentors engagement, which is independent of this workstream per DEC-122. Multi-tenancy routing fix slices in flight (SES-044, SES-045 plus remaining slices) continue independently and do not block this workstream per DEC-122.
